rationale.summary: The human asked for a unified random log sampling system to reduce high-QPS log volume, then followed up with config validation and sampler stress-test coverage. The walkthrough shows the PR replacing first-N rate limiting with a worker-authoritative random LogSampler, propagating structured sample config, migrating access logging to lazy facades, updating deployment/docs, and adding sampler UT/ST/performance coverage. The implementation intent is aligned with the requested logging sampler work, though the PR also includes broad API/protocol and facade migrations needed to carry the sampler through clients and workers.

keyPoints.point: Human intent was to implement a unified, configurable random sampler for access logs to reduce excessive logging at high QPS.
keyPoints.snippet: Use the superpowers brainstorming skill, then implement: feat(log): unified random log sampling system. We log too much at high QPS

keyPoints.point: Human follow-up explicitly requested sampler config validation and stress-test coverage.
keyPoints.snippet: feat(log): log sampler — config validation + ST coverage. Add gflag validation and a stress test for the sampler.

keyPoints.point: The PR replaces the previous limiter with a worker-authoritative random LogSampler and structured sampling config propagation.
keyPoints.snippet: This PR replaces first-N log rate limiting with a worker-authoritative random LogSampler, propagates structured sampling config

keyPoints.point: The PR adds sampler-focused unit, system, and performance coverage.

critique_ledger.title: EmbeddedConfig sample-rate setters have no regression coverage
critique_ledger.rationale: RequestSampleRate, AccessSampleRate, and DiagnosticSampleRate add the public embedded-worker configuration surface, including range filtering before values enter extraArgs, but no changed test exercises these setters through EmbeddedConfig or ParseCommandLineFlags. A typo in the flag names, invalid-rate handling, or failure to propagate embedded config to the sampler would pass the current sampler-only tests.

critique_ledger.title: Hot access paths now construct a much heavier recorder state even when sampling is off
critique_ledger.rationale: The default all-1.0 configuration disables sampling, but every converted client/worker access path still constructs ObjectAccessRecorder. That now includes ObjectAccessState with many std::string and std::function members before the actual operation starts, adding per-request constructor/destructor/cache footprint on hot paths such as KVClient::Create/Set/Get where the old AccessRecorder only held timing/name/type state and built RequestParam at record time.

critique_ledger.title: Snapshot publication logic is duplicated across config update paths
critique_ledger.rationale: The proto update path manually allocates, exchanges, retires old snapshots, and toggles samplerEnabled instead of reusing the same publication path as UpdateConfigFromFlags. Future changes to snapshot ownership, retirement, memory ordering, or enabled-state semantics must be made in multiple places, and this copy has already diverged from the mutex-scoped BuildAndPublishSnapshot flow.

critique_ledger.title: Dynamic sampler flag transaction is untested
critique_ledger.rationale: The new dynamic update path validates, commits, rolls back, and publishes request/access/diagnostic sampling rates as one transaction, but the diff only tests LogSampler::UpdateConfigFromFlags directly. A regression in UpdateFlagParameter, ValidateAndCommitSamplerFlags, rollback after a bad value, or explicit-state preservation would not be caught, so runtime config changes could silently leave clients/workers with the wrong sampling policy.

critique_ledger.title: Expire error path drops its access record
critique_ledger.rationale: When ExpireFromMaster returns an error inside the RetryWhenDeadlock callback, the function returns immediately from that branch and never reaches the new access.Result(rc).Record() call. The old code recorded the failed access before returning, so failed Expire requests can now lose their access log and only emit the recorder destructor warning.

critique_ledger.title: Sample-rate comments are malformed around derivation semantics
critique_ledger.rationale: The new Helm comments say `accessSampleRate or1.0` and `rate.0.0-1.0)`, which obscures the important rule that explicitly setting access or diagnostic rates disables derivation from requestSampleRate. Operators and future maintainers can misconfigure sampling by following the checked-in values documentation.

routed_questions.Logging Design And User Documentation: Does the documentation hunk that removes DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT provide an explicit migration path to the new request/access/diagnostic sample-rate configuration?
routed_questions.Configuration And Propagation Surface: Do the changed embedded-config and dynamic-flag hunks exercise and preserve validated request/access/diagnostic sample-rate propagation, including invalid-value rollback and worker-to-client sampler state?
routed_questions.Sampler Engine And Log Macros: Does the changed sampler publication hunk use one consistent snapshot ownership, retirement, memory-ordering, and enabled-state path for both flag and proto updates after deleting LogRateLimiter?
routed_questions.Access Recorder Facade Migration: Do the changed access-recorder facade hunks avoid unconditional heavy state construction on hot paths and still record exactly once on the Expire error return path?

Do the changed access-recorder facade hunks avoid unconditional heavy state construction on hot paths and still record exactly once on the Expire error return path?
